claims.claim: Cloudinary's Free plan includes 25 credits per month, where 1 credit equals 1,000 transformations OR 1 GB managed storage OR 1 GB net viewing bandwidth, measured on a rolling 30-day window.
Cloudinary's Plus paid tier list price in 2026 is approximately $89-99 per month.
Cloudinary's Free plan caps Admin API at 500 calls per hour.

Cloudinary's official documentation home is at cloudinary.com/documentation, and Cloudinary's REST API base path is api.cloudinary.com/v1_1/{cloud_name}/ with resources like /image/upload, /image/destroy, /resources/{resource_type}, and delivery URLs at res.cloudinary.com/{cloud_name}/.

Cloudinary's image optimization documentation describes f_auto + q_auto delivering automatic format and quality optimization with measurable bandwidth reduction (a worked AVIF-vs-JPEG example on the page is roughly 56% smaller).
WebP delivers around 25-34% smaller files than JPEG at equivalent visual quality per Google's lossy comparison study.
Cloudinary's auto-tagging output includes a confidence score; a 0.7 (70%) threshold is a common practical filter to balance precision/recall.
Cloudinary Free plan upload limits: 10 MB per image, 100 MB per video (raised on paid plans).
On the Free plan, 25 credits × 1,000 transformations/credit yields a notional cap of ~25,000 transformations/month if 100% of credits are spent on transformations.

Cloudinary delivers derived assets via CDN with long-lived public cache headers; default Cache-Control is public, max-age=31536000 (one year) for derived images, which functions as ≥30 days at the edge.

Common Hebrew-supporting fonts available through Cloudinary's l_text overlay (when uploaded as custom fonts or via Google Fonts integration): Heebo, Assistant, Rubik, David Libre, Frank Ruhl Libre, Suez One, Secular One. These are widely-used Hebrew web fonts on Google Fonts.
Cloudinary upload signatures use the current Unix timestamp and the server rejects requests where the timestamp is too far in the past or future (the practical tolerance window is roughly 1 hour).
Cloudinary paid plans raise the Admin API rate limit above the Free plan's 500 req/hour; the published Admin API documentation states paid plans begin at 2,000 requests per hour and rise per tier.
Cloudinary's URL Gen SDK is published in the cloudinary/js-url-gen GitHub repository.
Cloudinary's transformation parameter reference is documented at cloudinary.com/documentation/transformation_reference.
Cloudinary's generative AI transformations documentation lives at cloudinary.com/documentation/generative_ai_transformations.
Cloudinary's signed URLs and authenticated delivery are documented at cloudinary.com/documentation/control_access_to_media.
Cloudinary's video transformation reference is documented at cloudinary.com/documentation/video_transformation_reference.
Cloudinary maintains an R&D office in Petah Tikva at 20 Aharon Bart St., Building C, 2nd floor.
Cloudinary was founded in 2012 and its corporate HQ moved to San Jose, California in 2024.
